sections.paragraph: Ch. pron i.q. Heb. הֵם they, those, Dan 2:44, f. אִנִּין 7:17 . [“And in this passage for sunt, they are. The more regular fem. form would seem to be אִנִּין; but אִנִּין stands in all the editions; so e.g. Exo 1:19, Onk.-The form אִנּוּן comes from אִנְחוּן, and אִנֵּין or אִנִּין, from אִנְהֵן the demonstrative syllable אִן (ecce!) being prefixed. So also in the Talmud אִנְהוּ=חוּא. See under אָנֹכִי, and Heb. Gr. p. 292, 293, 13th edit.” Ges. add.] In Targg. also הִנּוּן f. הִנֵּין. Syr. ܗܶܢܘܰܢ and ܗܶܢܶܝܢ. (Incorrectly given by Winer, hi, hœ, which is אִלֵּין.)
